summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened the service with prayer, acknowledging that many in the congregation are navigating difficult seasons, or 'valleys,' in life. He introduced a two-week series titled 'God of the Valleys,' focusing on the timeless comfort of Psalm 23. The message centered on the truth that God is present and active with us in our lowest moments, not just on the mountaintops. Pastor Travis unpacked the metaphor of God as our Shepherd, explaining that like sheep, we are defenseless, prone to wander, and utterly dependent on His care and guidance. He emphasized that valleys are temporary passages we walk 'through,' not permanent destinations, and that God's presence is our ultimate comfort and protection. The sermon provided several key truths about God as our Shepherd: He is our provider who meets all our needs, our strength in times of weakness, and the one who restores our souls. Pastor Travis wove in personal testimony about stepping out in faith when God called him to leave a previous ministry, illustrating the principle of seeking God's kingdom first. He also addressed practical discipleship through the 'litmus tests' of time and tithe, challenging the congregation to examine their priorities. The message concluded with an invitation to trust in Jesus, the Good Shepherd, for salvation, restoration, and guidance through every valley of life.
mainPoints: God is our Shepherd; we are like dependent sheep who need His guidance, protection, and care in every season of life.
God is our comfort and provider in the valleys; He walks with us, defends us, and supplies all our needs according to His wisdom and timing.
God restores our souls and is our strength; our brokenness and weakness are opportunities for Him to display His power and bring healing and eternal life.

versesMentioned.text: The Lord is my shepherd, I lack nothing. He makes me lie down in green pastures, he leads me beside quiet waters, he refreshes my soul. He guides me along the right paths for his name’s sake. Even though I walk through the darkest valley, I will fear no evil, for you are with me; your rod and your staff, they comfort me. You prepare a table before me in the presence of my enemies. You anoint my head with oil; my cup overflows. Surely your goodness and love will follow me all the days of my life, and I will dwell in the house of the Lord forever.
I am the good shepherd; I know my sheep and my sheep know me—
My sheep listen to my voice; I know them, and they follow me.
And my God will meet all your needs according to the riches of his glory in Christ Jesus.

Then Jesus said to his disciples: 'Therefore I tell you, do not worry about your life, what you will eat; or about your body, what you will wear. For life is more than food, and the body more than clothes. Consider the ravens: They do not sow or reap, they have no storeroom or barn; yet God feeds them. And how much more valuable you are than birds! Who of you by worrying can add a single hour to your life? Since you cannot do this very little thing, why do you worry about the rest? Consider how the wild flowers grow. They do not labor or spin. Yet I tell you, not even Solomon in all his splendor was dressed like one of these. If that is how God clothes the grass of the field, which is here today, and tomorrow is thrown into the fire, how much more will he clothe you—you of little faith! And do not set your heart on what you will eat or drink; do not worry about it. For the pagan world runs after all such things, and your Father knows that you need them. But seek his kingdom, and these things will be given to you as well.'

Bring the whole tithe into the storehouse, that there may be food in my house. Test me in this,' says the Lord Almighty, 'and see if I will not throw open the floodgates of heaven and pour out so much blessing that there will not be room enough to store it.'

But he said to me, 'My grace is sufficient for you, for my power is made perfect in weakness.' Therefore I will boast all the more gladly about my weaknesses, so that Christ’s power may rest on me. That is why, for Christ’s sake, I delight in weaknesses, in insults, in hardships, in persecutions, in difficulties. For when I am weak, then I am strong.
Create in me a pure heart, O God, and renew a steadfast spirit within me.
For God so loved the world that he gave his one and only Son, that whoever believes in him shall not perish but have eternal life.

Monday.scripture: Psalm 23:1 (NIV) - 'The Lord is my shepherd, I lack nothing.'
Monday.action: Begin your day by consciously acknowledging God as your Shepherd. Write down one worry and consciously release it to His care.
Monday.prayer: Lord, thank you for being my Shepherd. Help me to trust that with you, I truly lack nothing. Guide my steps today.

Tuesday.scripture: Psalm 23:4 (NIV) - 'Even though I walk through the darkest valley, I will fear no evil, for you are with me; your rod and your staff, they comfort me.'
Tuesday.action: Identify a current 'valley' in your life. Spend 5 minutes in quiet reflection, reminding yourself that God is walking through it with you.
Tuesday.prayer: God, thank you for your promise to be with me. In my fear or anxiety, remind me of your comforting presence and protection.

Wednesday.scripture: Luke 12:31 (NIV) - 'But seek his kingdom, and these things will be given to you as well.'
Wednesday.action: Evaluate your schedule today. Intentionally put the 'big rock' of time with God (prayer, Scripture) first, before other tasks.
Wednesday.prayer: Father, forgive me for seeking other things first. Today, I choose to seek your kingdom. Align my desires and priorities with yours.

Thursday.scripture: Philippians 4:19 (NIV) - 'And my God will meet all your needs according to the riches of his glory in Christ Jesus.'
Thursday.action: Practice an act of trust in God's provision. This could be giving your tithe, helping someone in need, or simply choosing not to worry about a specific need.
Thursday.prayer: Provider God, I trust that you know my needs better than I do. I release my anxieties about provision to you and thank you for your faithfulness.

Friday.scripture: 2 Corinthians 12:9 (NIV) - 'My grace is sufficient for you, for my power is made perfect in weakness.'
Friday.action: Acknowledge an area where you feel weak, inadequate, or broken. Offer it to God in prayer, asking for His strength to be shown in that very place.
Friday.prayer: Lord, I am weak in this area. I invite you into my brokenness. Let your power be made perfect here, and use my story for your glory.

Saturday.scripture: Psalm 51:10 (NIV) - 'Create in me a pure heart, O God, and renew a steadfast spirit within me.'
Saturday.action: Take time for spiritual reflection and rest. Confess anything that hinders your relationship with God and receive His forgiveness and renewal.
Saturday.prayer: Restorer God, create in me a clean heart. Renew my spirit. Heal the broken places and restore to me the joy of your salvation.
